summary: Lead accepts M7-TUTORIAL-HINTS-ENCYCLOPEDIA-COMPLETE-001 after independent QA R2 ACCEPT. The task adds Beta tutorial, contextual hints, and encyclopedia client/read-model surfaces; QA R1 requested repair for accepted M7 content-label drift; writer R2 mechanically aligned the guidance fixture with accepted content-source/m7-beta-scenarios/beta-scenario-person-event-set.json and added drift-guard tests; QA R2 accepted. This acceptance does not close the task until the branch enters origin/main.

tests_run.result: 20 tests passed including accepted-content drift guard.
format, lint, typecheck, and architecture checks passed.

decisions: ACCEPT after independent QA R2 ACCEPT; task remains not CLOSED until PR integration into origin/main.
M7 guidance now mechanically aligns with accepted content-source labels/review states/counts and tests fail on drift.
No package dependency or pnpm-lock.yaml change was introduced.
Formal content lock remains a future Human Gate and is not created by this task.
risks: Generated client fixture must be mechanically synced if accepted content-source changes; the new drift guard blocks unsynced changes.
Existing Vite/Storybook chunk warnings remain non-blocking.

requested_action: Open PR, wait for CI, merge to main, then close M7-TUTORIAL-HINTS-ENCYCLOPEDIA-COMPLETE-001 and continue to the next dependency-ready M7 task.
